$comment: Single source of truth for findings.json structure (see VALIDATION-AND-REPORTING.md Round 4). validate-findings.cjs reads this file directly and interprets it — there is no second copy of these rules to keep in sync. Two constraints can't be expressed in this JSON-Schema subset and live in the validator's semantic layer: (1) a confirmed finding needs a non-empty concrete_cost; (2) reusability-hoist and copy-paste findings both need >=2 evidence sites (duplication is inherently multi-site) and a drift_test, and no confirmed reuse/duplication finding may have drift_test.verdict == 'normal-to-diverge'.

oneOf.description: Confirmed structural finding that clears the falsifiability anchor (a named concrete cost).

description.description: What the structural problem is, in plain terms.

concrete_cost.description: THE ANCHOR. The specific future change this structure makes expensive/dangerous, OR the bug it has already caused. If this is a taste statement ('cleaner', 'violates SRP') with no named change and no bug, the finding is invalid — reject it instead.

note.description: What this location shows (a duplication site, a cycle member, the unused export, etc.).

evidence.description: Objective, verifiable facts. For reusability-hoist and copy-paste, list every drifted/duplicated site (>=2).

drift_test.description: Required for reusability-hoist and copy-paste findings. The DRY-is-about-knowledge test.

verdict.description: A confirmed reuse finding must be 'bug-if-diverge'. 'normal-to-diverge' means incidental duplication — do not report it as confirmed.

strategy.description: The smallest change that removes the cost. Do not propose abstraction beyond what the cost justifies.

target.description: For hoist findings: the canonical version and the designated shared destination.

classification.description: fix-now: cheap and in the path of active work. tracked-debt: real cost, needs its own effort. accepted: cost low enough to live with, recorded so it isn't re-litigated.

reason.description: How much code / how many call sites — cite counts.
How actively this code changes — cite churn rank.
Note anything you couldn't fully trace.

oneOf.description: Rejected finding — investigated and dropped. Kept so later runs don't re-derive it.

reason.description: Which test it failed: no real cost (taste), normal-to-diverge (incidental duplication), speculative abstraction, framework idiom, false substrate fact, or a factual error found in Round 5.
